name: Lean-Away Dumbbell Lateral Raise
resistance_profile: Holding a support and leaning away tilts the torso so the dumbbell's torque stays high at the bottom, shifting peak tension into the lengthened / stretched portion of the range.
cues: Lean away and let the arm hang across the body
Lead with the elbow to shoulder height
Control the descent into a full stretch
execution_steps: Grab a squat rack or sturdy upright with one hand and hold a dumbbell in the other, that arm hanging down.
Step your feet close to the upright and lean your torso away so the working arm hangs slightly across the body's midline.
With a small bend in the elbow, raise the dumbbell out to the side up to about shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, keeping the trap down.
Lower slowly all the way into the stretch, feeling the side delt lengthen, then repeat.
Finish all reps on one side, then switch arms.
good_when: You want to emphasise the stretched, lengthened position of the side delt
You only have dumbbells but want a stretch-biased raise
bad_when: No stable upright is available to lean from
The deep bottom stretch irritates the shoulder
common_errors: Not leaning enough, losing the bottom-range tension
Swinging the dumbbell with body momentum
Shrugging the traps at the top
progressions: Increase the lean to deepen the bottom stretch
regressions: Stand upright for a standard dumbbell lateral raise
Reduce the range or the load
